title: Rule Registry Schema
description: Canonical JSON schema for world-authored rule entries in the Frozen World Bundle. Each entry binds a world-flavored rule description to an engine substrate procedure via stable IDs. Content-independent — no game-system-specific vocabulary in field names.

RuleRegistry.title: RuleRegistry
RuleRegistry.description: Top-level registry of all world-authored rule entries. Maps content_id → RuleEntry. Sorted by content_id for deterministic serialization.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this registry file (major.minor)

world_id.description: World identity hash — must match world_manifest.json world_id

compiler_version.description: Version of the world compiler that produced this registry

entry_count.description: Total number of entries (for quick integrity check)

entries.description: All rule entries, sorted by content_id

RuleEntry.title: RuleEntry
RuleEntry.description: A single world-authored rule binding. References an engine procedure by stable procedure_id. Adds world-flavored text, parameters, and provenance. The engine procedure defines WHAT happens; the rule entry defines HOW it is described in this world.

content_id.description: Content pack identifier (e.g., 'spell.fireball', 'feat.power_attack'). Uses the mechanical_id namespace from canonical_ids.py: domain.canonical_name.

procedure_id.description: Stable reference to the engine substrate procedure that resolves this rule (e.g., 'proc.ranged_burst_damage', 'proc.melee_attack'). The procedure exists in the engine; the compiler does NOT define it.

rule_type.description: Classification of this rule within the content taxonomy

world_name.description: World-flavored display name (from lexicon). Must match lexicon.json entry for this content_id.

tier.description: Implementation tier. tier_1 = fully resolved by engine. tier_2 = schema defined, resolution deferred. tier_3 = placeholder slot. stub = explicitly absent with marker.

parameters.description: Mechanical parameters authored by the content pack and bound by the world compiler. These parameterize the engine procedure — the compiler does NOT invent mechanics.

text_slots.description: World-authored descriptive text for this rule. Frozen at compile time.

tags.description: Classification tags for indexing and search (e.g., 'fire', 'area_effect', 'melee', 'defensive')

ref.description: Reference to the prerequisite (content_id for feat, 'str:13' for ability score, etc.)

display.description: World-flavored display text for this prerequisite

prerequisites.description: Prerequisites for this rule, if applicable. Empty array if none.

supersedes.description: content_ids of rules this entry supersedes (e.g., improved version of a feat). Empty if none.

RuleParameters.title: RuleParameters
RuleParameters.description: Mechanical parameters that configure the engine procedure. These come from the content pack; the compiler binds them to the world entry. The schema is deliberately flexible — different rule_types have different parameter shapes.

area_shape.description: Area of effect shape, if applicable

area_radius_ft.description: Area radius in feet, if applicable

custom.description: Rule-type-specific parameters not covered by the standard fields. Keys are parameter names, values are primitives or arrays.

RuleTextSlots.title: RuleTextSlots
RuleTextSlots.description: World-authored descriptive text for this rule. All text is generated at compile time and frozen. No copyrighted content. No coaching or tactical advice.

rulebook_description.description: Full rulebook entry text (generated from Layer A + Layer B). This is what the player sees when they 'open the rulebook.'

short_description.description: One-line summary for indices and tooltips

flavor_text.description: World-flavored lore text (optional, frozen at compile)

mechanical_summary.description: Concise mechanical description using world vocabulary (e.g., 'Launches a bolt of searing energy that detonates in a 20-foot burst')

RuleProvenance.title: RuleProvenance
RuleProvenance.description: Provenance record for a world-authored rule entry. Traces the entry back to compile inputs.

compiler_version.description: Version of the compiler that generated this entry

seed_used.description: The derived seed used for this entry's generation (from the rulebook_seed derivation)

content_pack_id.description: Content pack that provided the mechanical template

template_ids.description: IDs of content pack templates used to generate this entry

llm_output_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the LLM output used to generate text slots (for determinism verification). Null if no LLM was used.

description: This schema defines the Rule Registry: world-authored rule entries that bind engine procedures to world-flavored names, parameters, and descriptions. Each entry references a stable procedure_id in the engine substrate. The compiler generates text and tags; it does NOT define or modify engine procedures.
